key_insight: A unified multiphysics phase-field model links anisotropic, rate-dependent fracture and piezoresistive conductivity through fiber orientation, enabling damage inference in short carbon fiber composites.
engineering_relevance: It provides a physics-based route for real-time structural health monitoring of SCFRP parts without direct mechanical sensing.
method: Finite-deformation phase-field fracture coupled with viscoelastic-viscoplastic constitutive behavior.
Second-order fiber orientation tensor used to define crack resistance anisotropy and conduction pathways.
Eight-electrode EIT data fed to a neural network to predict normalized crack length and compliance.
evidence_signal: The feedforward neural network achieved R2 = 0.99 on held-out configurations, indicating strong generalization across microstructure variations.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate orientation-tensor and conductivity-model calibration, so transfer to new composite layups or loading rates may require retraining or validation.
paper_title: Data-Driven Structural Health Monitoring of Short Carbon Fiber-Reinforced Polymer Composites via Multiphysics Phase-Field Simulation
